channel.title: General (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "General" - General Discussion

item.title: SCP Cosplay suggestions
item.content:encoded:
So in my country there is no Halloween but a holiday later in the year called Yom Kipur (יום כיפור)
and at that holiday you dress up as anything you want and generally celebrate in school, work, parties etc… So for this year i wanted to cosplay as a humanoid SCP of course but I don't know which one. Send me suggestions and I will pick the most interesting ones. Thanks!

item.title: Story’s about GOC
item.content:encoded: I really like the concept of the GOC but I can’t seem to find a lot of good stories involving them. Know any?

item.title: weird question about the website
item.content:encoded: is there any way to turn on a dark mode for the website?

item.title: what would make something a -j
item.content:encoded: i see -j in some SCPs what does it mean? is it just a name like SCP-xxxx-2 or is it something else?

item.title: Summary: What Remarkable Achievements You've Made in 2020?
item.description: Either in this Wiki or IRL or other (if you think they're great). Countdown!

item.title: Let's say if SCP was real, which department would you choose?
item.description: Basically what department would you choose to be in the SCP foundation.
item.content:encoded:
There are different SCP foundation departments and if you don't know them, these are the departments of the SCP foundation. Now, let's say, in an alternate universe, SCP DID exist and you had the option to choose to be one of the departments. Which one would YOU choose? Would you choose to test SCPs? Would you like to manage the SCP foundation? Would you like to secure the facility? Or would you like to contain SCPs? You choose down in the comments.

In my opinion, I would choose to be a AD

item.title: What is the most absolutely disgusting, stomach churning SCP you've read?
item.description: SCPs that are hard to sleep or eat after reading.
item.content:encoded: My personal most disgusting one is SCP-835, one of my favorites. Second is SCP-3003.

item.title: How are the Contests Chosen and Decided Exactly?
item.description: Who makes the decisions on contests? The admins?
item.content:encoded: I'm just inquiring on how the contests are actually decided. Not the winners of these contents, but rather how is theme and rules are chosen for each one. Are the admins in charge of that or is there some other process I'm not aware of?

item.title: Help
item.description: I made an application for the SCP Wiki and it got accepted. I tried to make an article but it doesn’t let me. I was signed in so it should let me. Can someone help me out?
item.content:encoded:
Exactly what the title says. A message comes up saying:

Please note:

Sorry, you can not create a new page in this category. Only members of this site, site administrators and perhaps selected moderators are allowed to do it.

item.title: Scandinavians, look here! - The Scandinavian Branch (ND) seeks recruits!
item.description: Recruitment thread for the Scandinavian Branch (ND).
item.content:encoded:
Hello!

We in the Scandinavian branch (ND) of the SCP Foundation are looking for new members. If you happen to speak Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, or even Faroese, you are more than welcome to come join the ND Wiki and the ND Discord server!

Because the ND branch is still in need of further development, we're especially interested in people with experience in any of the following areas:
- Moderation and Administration
- CSS coding and other technical know-how
- Translation
- Creative writing in Danish, Faroese, Norwegian, and/or Swedish

If you have experience in the two first areas (in addition to speaking one of the four languages) and wish to apply that knowledge in ND, please contact me directly with a short resume of your experiences.

Of course you're welcome as a member even if you don't already have a background in any of these areas. We need all the help we can get.
So if you're interested, come on over to http://scp-nd.wikidot.com/ and sign up!
You're also welcome to join our Discord server at: https://discord.gg/QTsMfgAu

item.title: Whatever happened to to that roleplay website
item.description: Some people awhile ago said something about an SCP roleplay website and I want to know what happened to it
item.content:encoded: Okay while trying to find the thread where they mentioned that they were going to make it I found the website it’s dead, check it out if you want three-portlands.wikidot.com I tried so hard to get the password to join the site but I couldn’t find it.

item.title: Best trippy screw skips?
item.description: Basically any serious article with fever dream moments integrated-but-sort-of-still-breaking with the format.
item.content:encoded: Title and summary says it all (but if you wanna know what kind of "trippy" I'm talking about, 3999, The 12 Days Of Christine, eternal sunshine in the spotless mind, and Interstellar come the closest to portraying it).

item.title: Christmas SCPs
item.description: Looking for christmassy scips, recommendations and suggestions.
item.content:encoded:
Hi. I'm looking to do readings (https://www.youtube.com/c/ReadOut) of Christmas SCP items. These are the one's I've got:

784 - Christmas Cheer
1225 - The Worst Christmas
1933 - Bailey's Santa
2497 - Home for the CactusNights
2536 - The Gift That Keeps On Giving
3193 - Christmas War
3355 - St. Nick
4255 - Santa Claus, The Time Traveler
4666 - Yule Man

So, are there any (including deleted ones) that I've missed?

item.title: Any scp articles suggestions for someone new to scp?
item.description: I'm just pretty new to this and want to know what you guys think I should read, like any essentials in your opinion?
item.content:encoded: Hey guys, I'm pretty new to this whole scp thing, anyone mind telling me some of their favorite articles or any they find essential? I have only read 963, 5040, 173 and 1471, I would love some suggestions!

item.title: Recommendations wanted
item.description: I would love some recommendations for articles to start with.
item.content:encoded:
Hello there!
I’m pretty new to SCP. I only found out about it a couple days ago (which is surprising since I absolutely love things like SCP).
I need some recommendations for articles to start with, as the lore is vast and impossible to take in all at once. Any recommendations?
Thanks!

item.title: Research Project Help
item.description: Looking for someone willing to do an interview for a graduate school project
item.content:encoded:
Hello,
My name is Rachel and I'm a graduate student at the University of Oregon. I study folklore and folk groups. I'm studying the SCP Foundation for a class about art and how it functions in society. I'm looking for someone to interview about their work for the SCP Foundation and the community in general. This interview would not be published and would only be seen by me and my professor. Please contact me if you are interested.

item.title: That Halloween Spirit!
item.description: We've really gone for it this year.
item.content:encoded:
Most bone-chilling greetings to ye! I have returned from my absence to do my yearly Halloween round up, and this year I am most impressed.

The site is dressed up black and orange, the truest colours of Halloween (plus purple). No Halloween feature, but honestly the look is impressive enough to me. Insidious pumpkin face on the logo, serial killer font, Spook, Creep, Pester! I love it. Outside of the main site the Halloween spirit is strong as well. Google Translate still doesn't work for Chinese, so ¯\_(ツ)_/¯, but elsewhere it's in full force!
The German wiki keeps on giving as it does every year (makes sense I suppose, theirs is the home of the Grimm tales), having just ended another Halloween competition, with a great spooky theme to boot.
Sadly, the French didn't do anything this year, though they do have a neat competition on the theme of errors, with a very clever theme rearranging the widgets on the page.
The Italian branch is also having a Halloween contest. No special page theme, but they have found a way to implement a dark mode, which is nice (possible feature of Project Foundation?).
The Polish branch didn't want to be left out and have a competition of their own, with the interesting theme of articles that have previously been deleted (meta-zombies, anybody?). Ressurection of the Removed, Translate calls it.
And the Spanish-speaking branch, oh my, they have outdone themselves. Not just a cool page theme with an animated banner, not just some nice art on the main page, but a skeleton vs pumpkin story-fighting competition, where the two sides go head-to-head represented by gifs of a skeleton head and a pumpkin breathing fire at each other! Top marks.

Small notes about the other sites.
– hide block
  • The Brazilian branch has a neat theme, but it doesn't seem to be Halloween related. They also have an animated logo, where the SCP logo is the wheels of a car, which is… cool?
  • Why do the Russian and Ukrainian branches have different page layouts to everyone else?
  • In a funny example of Google Translate's imperfection, the Ukrainian version of 'Secure, Contain, Protect' has been translated to 'Protect, Hold, Protect'.

Back in the English-speaking part of the internet, we have social media.
YouTuber TheeSherm is putting on a 24-hour Halloween charity livestream again, live right now! He's gathered a bunch of SCP authors and creators and they'll be doing just SCP stuff for 24 hours, in aid of an LGBT centre. Details here.
I can't tell if the SCP subreddit is being especially spooky today, because honestly they're always like that. But the colours are especially black and orange today, so perhaps they did put in some extra effort.

That's all I have for you. If I missed anything, please comment it yourself. I leave you again with the Word of the Day. Today it's 'eldritch', but I'm sure most of you already know that word, being on this site and all, so instead I'll give you yesterday's, which is one of my favourite words ever: 'gloaming'.

Gloaming, noun: twilight, dusk, specifically the part of dusk where the sun has set but the sky is not completely dark as its glow is still visible on the horizon; 'The Gloaming Wildwoods'1.

Happy Halloween, and stay safe!

Footnotes
1. Many points if you get this reference

item.title: What are your two favorite articles on the SCP wiki?
item.content:encoded: I like SCP-2935 and SCP-TTKU-J.

item.title: Snippets From Chat You Had To Save For All Time #17
item.description: The last one was at 173 posts haha funny
item.content:encoded:
Calibri_Bold> How are you doing?
11:50 PM <The_DodoDevil> tbh, there's a lot of variety with wine
11:50 PM <•TI-84> Oh cool thanks
11:50 PM I’m alright
11:50 PM Full of pasta
11:50 PM <The_DodoDevil> Like personally not a fan of white, but I do love a resiling
11:50 PM <Calibri_Bold> TI-84: Ah, I was sorry to hear about your chef.
11:50 PM <The_DodoDevil> *riesling
11:50 PM <Calibri_Bold> It's very sad that he…
11:50 PM <•TI-84> What
11:50 PM <Calibri_Bold> …Pasta way.
11:50 PM <•TI-84> Am I going to have to kick you
11:50 PM Yes
11:51 PM <DrMoned> lmao
11:51 PM ← Calibri_Bold (oid.em.saw.ti.tub.ksamtsoh.a.detcepxe.uoy|643693diu#oid.em.saw.ti.tub.ksamtsoh.a.detcepxe.uoy|643693diu) was kicked by •TI-84

item.title: Im kind of bummed rn
item.description: Idk just want someone to tell me I got this
item.content:encoded:
Eh, I just have been really bummed as of late. I've been feeling much more lazy, and overall have been much less productive. This may be kind of selfish, but right now, I just want someone to tell me I got this.

~Yellow (by Coldplay)
